title: Two Sum
preview: Find two numbers in a given array that add up to a specific target value, and return their indices. Essentially, you need to efficiently locate the pair that satisfies the sum requirement.

title: LRU Cache
preview: Design a data structure that acts as a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. Implement methods to get and put key-value pairs, evicting the least recently used entry when the cache is full.

title: Merge Intervals
preview: You are given a collection of intervals. Merge all overlapping intervals into a single interval and return the result.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
preview: You're given daily stock prices. Find the maximum profit you can make by buying and selling the stock once. You must buy before you sell.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
preview: Find the length of the longest substring within a given string that contains no repeating characters. The substring must be contiguous.

title: Valid Parentheses
preview: Determine if a string containing parentheses is valid. A valid string has matching and properly nested opening and closing parentheses.

title: Number of Islands
preview: Given a grid representing land and water, count the number of distinct islands. An island is formed by connected land cells.

title: Group Anagrams
preview: Given a list of words, group together all the anagrams. Anagrams are words that contain the same letters rearranged.

title: Trapping Rain Water
preview: Given an array representing the height of bars, calculate how much water can be trapped between them after rain. Think about finding the maximum height to the left and right of each bar.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
preview: Find the longest palindromic substring within a given string. A palindromic substring reads the same forwards and backward.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
preview: Find the k most frequent elements in a given array of numbers. Return the top k elements based on their frequency.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
preview: Find the median of two sorted arrays. The arrays can be of different sizes, and you should aim for an efficient solution.

title: 3Sum
preview: Find all unique triplets in an array that sum to zero. You'll need to consider the potential for duplicate numbers and avoid duplicate triplets in your solution.

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
preview: Determine the total number of continuous subarrays within a given array that sum up to a specific target value, k.

title: Merge k Sorted Lists
preview: You are given k sorted lists. Combine all of their elements into a single sorted list and return it.

title: Longest Common Prefix
preview: Find the longest string that is a prefix of all strings in a given array. If no common prefix exists, return an empty string.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
preview: You're given a sorted array that has been rotated, and a target value. Find the index of the target value in the rotated array, or return -1 if it's not present.

title: Maximum Subarray
preview: Find the contiguous subarray within a given array of numbers which has the largest sum. Return the sum of this subarray.

title: Rotate Image
preview: You are given a square matrix representing an image. Rotate the image by 90 degrees clockwise in-place.

title: Spiral Matrix
preview: Traverse a given matrix in a spiral pattern, returning all elements in the order you visit them. Essentially, walk around the matrix layer by layer, adding elements to a list.

title: Add Two Numbers
preview: You are given two linked lists, each representing a non-negative integer. Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
preview: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array. Note that it is the kth largest element in sorted order, not the kth distinct element.

title: Generate Parentheses
preview: Given a number 'n', generate all possible well-formed (balanced) parentheses strings of length 2*n. For instance, if n=3, you need to create all valid combinations like '((()))', '(()())', etc.

title: Rotting Oranges
preview: You are given a grid of oranges, some fresh, some rotten.  Rotten oranges contaminate adjacent fresh oranges; find the minimum time until all oranges are rotten.

title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
preview: Given a sorted array, find the first and last position of a given target value. If the target is not found, return [-1, -1].

title: Merge Sorted Array
preview: You are given two sorted integer arrays. Merge the second array into the first one, maintaining the sorted order.

title: Roman to Integer
preview: Convert a Roman numeral string into its corresponding integer value. The input string will represent a valid Roman numeral.

title: House Robber
preview: You are a robber planning to rob houses along a street. Maximize your loot, but be careful - adjacent houses have connected security systems that will trigger an alarm if you rob them both.

title: Palindrome Number
preview: Determine whether an integer is a palindrome. That is, it reads the same forwards and backward.

title: Move Zeroes
preview: Given an array, rearrange it so that all zeros are moved to the end while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.

title: Container With Most Water
preview: You are given an array representing the heights of vertical lines. Find two lines that, together with the x-axis, form a container that holds the most water.

title: Meeting Rooms II
preview: You are given a list of meeting time intervals. Determine the minimum number of conference rooms required to accommodate all the meetings without any overlap.

title: Coin Change
preview: Given a set of coin denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins needed to reach that amount. You can assume you have an unlimited supply of each coin type.

title: Valid Anagram
preview: Determine if two strings are anagrams of each other, meaning they contain the same characters in a different order. You need to check if one string can be rearranged to form the other.

title: Product of Array Except Self
preview: Calculate a new array where each element is the product of all numbers in the original array, excluding the number at that index. Do this efficiently without using division.

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
preview: Given an unsorted array of integers, find the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. These consecutive numbers do not need to be adjacent in the original array.

title: Koko Eating Bananas
preview: Koko wants to eat all the bananas in some piles within a certain time. Find the minimum eating speed that allows her to finish on time.

title: Jump Game
preview: You are given an array where each element represents your maximum jump length at that position. Determine if you can reach the last index starting from the first index.

title: Integer to Roman
preview: Convert a given integer into its Roman numeral representation. You need to understand the mapping between integer values and Roman symbols to perform the conversion accurately.

title: Course Schedule
preview: Determine if it is possible to finish all courses given prerequisite relationships between them. Essentially, detect if a cycle exists in the course dependencies.

title: Minimum Window Substring
preview: Find the smallest substring within a larger string that contains all the characters of a target string. You need to locate the minimum window that satisfies this condition.

title: Reverse Linked List
preview: You are given the head of a singly linked list. Reverse the list and return the new head.

title: Next Permutation
preview: Given a sequence of numbers, find the next lexicographically greater permutation of the numbers. If no greater permutation exists, rearrange the numbers into the smallest possible permutation (ascending order).

title: Sliding Window Maximum
preview: Find the maximum value within each sliding window of a given size as it moves across an array.

title: Course Schedule II
preview: You're given a list of courses and their prerequisites. Determine a valid order in which you can take all the courses, respecting the dependencies. If it's impossible to finish all courses, indicate that.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
preview: You're given two sorted lists. Combine them into a single sorted list containing all elements from both original lists.

title: Valid Palindrome
preview: Determine if a given string is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases. Essentially, check if the string reads the same forwards and backward after removing non-alphanumeric characters and converting to lowercase.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
preview: Given a sorted array, remove the duplicate elements in-place such that each unique element appears only once. Return the new length of the array.

title: Find Peak Element
preview: Find a peak element in an array, where a peak is greater than its neighbors. You only need to return the index of any one peak element if multiple exist.

title: Word Ladder
preview: Transform one word into another, one letter at a time, using only words from a given dictionary. Find the shortest sequence of words to reach the target.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
preview: Maximize your profit by buying and selling a stock multiple times. You're given an array representing stock prices, and can make as many transactions as you want.

title: Subsets
preview: Given a set of distinct integers, your task is to generate all possible subsets (the power set). The solution should not contain duplicate subsets.

title: Rotate Array
preview: Given an array, rotate the elements to the right by a specified number of steps. The goal is to modify the original array in-place with the rotated elements.

title: Min Stack
preview: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time. You need to implement a stack with an additional function to efficiently get the minimum value.

title: Word Break
preview: Determine if a given string can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of words from a dictionary. You are given a string and a dictionary of words; can you break the string down using words from the dictionary?

title: Word Search
preview: Determine if a given word can be constructed from letters in a 2D board, where letters can be used horizontally or vertically. Each letter can only be used once in the word.

title: Contains Duplicate
preview: Determine if any value appears at least twice in an array of numbers. Return true if duplicates exist; otherwise, return false.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
preview: Find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence within a given array of numbers. A subsequence doesn't need to be contiguous, but the elements must maintain their original order.

title: Reorganize String
preview: Rearrange the characters in a given string such that no two adjacent characters are the same. If such an arrangement is possible, return it; otherwise, return an empty string.

title: Climbing Stairs
preview: You are climbing a staircase with n steps. Find how many distinct ways you can climb to the top, given that you can climb one or two steps at a time.

title: String Compression
preview: Compress a given string by replacing consecutive repeating characters with the character followed by the count of repetitions. If the compressed string isn't shorter, return the original.

title: Sort Colors
preview: You're given an array containing only 0s, 1s, and 2s. Sort the array in place so that elements of the same value are grouped together.

title: Jump Game II
preview: You are given an array where each element represents the maximum jump length from that position. Find the minimum number of jumps required to reach the last index.

title: Decode String
preview: The problem involves decoding a string that follows a specific encoding rule: k[encoded_string], where the encoded_string inside the square brackets is repeated exactly k times. Your task is to decode the string and return the decoded string.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
preview: Find the maximum sum of any path in a binary tree, where a path can start and end at any node. The path must contain at least one node.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
preview: You're given a linked list where each node has a regular 'next' pointer and a 'random' pointer. Create a deep copy of this list, preserving both the next and random pointer relationships.

title: Valid Sudoku
preview: Determine if a given 9x9 Sudoku board is valid. Check if each row, column, and 3x3 subgrid contains the digits 1-9 without repetition.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
preview: Given a string of digits, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent, using the typical phone keypad mapping. The goal is to generate all valid combinations in any order.

title: Daily Temperatures
preview: Given a list of daily temperatures, find how many days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. Return a list where each element represents the waiting days for each day's temperature.

title: Candy
preview: Distribute candies to children standing in a line such that each child with a higher rating gets more candies than their neighbors. Find the minimum number of candies needed.

title: Pow(x, n)
preview: Calculate x raised to the power of n. Be mindful of potential edge cases and optimize for efficiency, as a naive approach might not be sufficient.

title: LFU Cache
preview: Design and implement a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache. The cache should efficiently store key-value pairs, evicting the least frequently used items when it reaches capacity.

title: Simplify Path
preview: Given an absolute file path (Unix style), simplify it by resolving redundant separators, directory names, and navigating back up the directory structure. Return the simplified canonical path.

title: Basic Calculator
preview: Implement a basic calculator to evaluate a simple expression string. The expression can contain parentheses, addition, and subtraction.

title: Majority Element
preview: Find the element that appears more than n/2 times in a given array of size n. Essentially, you need to identify the majority element.
